one_time_description: Reconciliation agreement with Rio Tinto-IOC mining company to compensate impacted families; funds held in restricted account
economic_notes: ITUM holds equity interests in multiple commercial enterprises including Innu Construction, L'Immobilière Montagnaise SEC, Société de Développement Économique (SDEUM), Société en commandite NISHK, Transport ferroviaire Tshieutini inc., Tshiuetin SEC, Groupe Umek SEC, Les Pêcheries Nitshimau SENC, Pêcheries Uapan SEC, Atautshuap SEC, Société en commandite APUIAT, 9321-1654 Québec inc., 9392-8711 Québec inc., Centre Culturel Innu de la Rivière Misha-Shipu Inc., 3201015 Canada inc. Signed reconciliation and collaboration agreement with Rio Tinto-IOC in October 2020.
notes: Financial statements are in French and prepared under Canadian Public Sector Accounting Standards (PSAS). Combined statements consolidate all ITUM-controlled entities except commercial enterprises, which are accounted for using modified equity method. 15 commercial enterprise investments held at equity. Significant deferred revenue of $16.8M in 2021 vs $6.3M in 2020, largely due to SAC infrastructure and health funding. Prior year adjustments of -$655,894 recorded. Restricted cash includes housing reserve ($2,031,382), health reserve ($159,692), and families fund ($1,207,110 from Rio Tinto-IOC agreement). Revenue and expense detail by program not broken out in the extracted text — annexes referenced but not fully available. ISC/SAC funding cannot be precisely isolated from available text. The schedule of remuneration for chiefs and councillors is referenced (page 45) but amounts not included in extracted text.
